implementation_summary: Implemented the full update-detection runtime: (1) useUpdateChecker hook with 5-min interval, immediate check on visibilitychange/focus, cache-busting fetch, fail-soft error handling, and a latched updateAvailable boolean. Exports shouldShowUpdate pure function for direct unit testing. Skips setup entirely when NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION is falsy (local dev safety). (2) UpdateBanner component modelled on OfflineBanner.tsx — Chakra Alert, session-only dismissed state, Reload/dismiss testid attributes, no hardcoded strings. (3) i18n.ts extended with updateBanner section in Copy type and both en/de language objects. (4) _app.tsx wired: hook called in AppShell, UpdateBanner rendered as sibling above Layout, window.__BUILD_VERSION exposed for e2e introspection. (5) 15 unit tests covering shouldShowUpdate pure logic and all fetch fail-soft branches. (6) 7 e2e tests covering banner appearance, same-version suppression, network fail-soft, dismiss, non-blocking UX, and visibilitychange trigger.
mocks_introduced: globalThis.fetch mocked via Object.defineProperty in useUpdateChecker.test.ts (restored in afterEach)
typecheck: clean — npx tsc --noEmit produced no errors

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The unit test strategy required care: the hook uses useEffect/useState which need a DOM unavailable in Vitest without jsdom. The solution — exporting shouldShowUpdate as a pure function and simulating the fetch-path logic inline in tests — is clean but required understanding that the fetch-path tests were not calling the hook at all, just re-implementing its internal conditional logic in test scope. The spec was explicit about this pattern (via unreadStore.test.ts reference), which made it tractable.
retrospective.surprised_by: The 'same version' e2e test had a non-obvious dependency: the baked NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION in the dev server bundle could not be known at test-authoring time. The solution (expose via window.__BUILD_VERSION and read it from the page after navigation) was specified in the story brief, which resolved this correctly. The order of operations in the test (goto first, then route intercept, then evaluate focus) would have been incorrect without reading the spec carefully — route intercept must be set before goto for the initial mount fetch.
